description: A person who 'ruemi' lies with the body horizontal or reclined against a supporting surface. They might sleep, read, receive care, or merely look under the bed from there. Neither 'therilu' (rest) nor 'nulae' (sleep) is part of the posture itself.
articulatory_notes: Make a tap or short trill at the alveolar ridge and move into rounded high-back 'u'. Give stressed middle-height front 'e' a separate syllable. Close the lips for voiced 'm', keep the resonance in the nose, and finish with high-front 'i'.
sound_symbolism: Rounded 'u' opens across the 'u.e' hiatus, giving the middle of the word time to lower without a rush. Final 'mi' closes the lips lightly and lets the sound come to a quiet stop.
usage_notes: Inchoative 'pa ruemi' means to lie down. Without 'pa', 'ruemi' can present the horizontal or reclining posture as already held, and a locative preposition can mark its support.

examples.translation: I lie down on the bed.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Lying down completes the traditional set of four postures in which mindfulness can be practised. The posture does not guarantee mindfulness; it merely keeps the practice possible when the body is horizontal.

semantic_domains.activity: holding or entering a horizontal or reclining posture
semantic_domains.physical: bodily posture supported along more than the feet or seat
semantic_domains.spatial: horizontal or reclined position against a surface
